docs.summary: Represents a physical or mailing address, decomposed into street, city, state, postal code, and country components.
docs.remarks: is a record class providing value-based equality and immutability by default, making it suitable for use as an embedded value object within aggregate roots such as . The  field uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes rather than full country names to ensure consistent, locale-independent storage.

parameters.Street: The street address, including house or building number (e.g., "123 Main St").

docs.remarks: Centralizing route paths in a static class ensures that controllers, clients, and tests reference the same values, reducing the risk of routing mismatches.

A generic envelope for API responses, providing a consistent wire format that wraps the data payload alongside success/failure status and metadata.
docs.remarks: All API endpoints should return  to give clients a uniform structure for deserialization. The  flag allows clients to branch on success or failure without inspecting HTTP status codes, while  provides server-side timing for debugging and cache-invalidation purposes. For error responses,  will be  and will contain a human-readable error description. For richer error bodies, consider returning as the  payload.

docs.summary: Creates an error response with the specified message.
docs.returns: A new  marked as unsuccessful.

docs.remarks: When ,  contains the error description and  is .

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC timestamp indicating when the server generated this response. Useful for debugging latency and cache invalidation.
Base class for entities that require audit tracking.
docs.remarks: All entities inheriting from  automatically receive a unique identifier and timestamp fields for creation and last modification. The  and  properties record the identity of the user who performed the respective action.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC date and time when the entity was created.
Gets or sets the identifier of the user who created the entity.
Gets or sets the unique identifier of the entity.
Gets or sets the UTC date and time when the entity was last updated.
Gets or sets the identifier of the user who last updated the entity.
Specifies the preferred communication channel for contacting a customer.
docs.remarks: This enumeration is used by the notification subsystem to select the appropriate delivery mechanism. It is stored as its integer value in the database and serialised as a string in JSON responses.

enumMembers.name: Email
enumMembers.description: Contact via email. This is the default channel.

enumMembers.name: Phone
enumMembers.description: Contact via a voice phone call.

enumMembers.name: Sms
enumMembers.description: Contact via short message service (SMS / text message).

docs.summary: Represents a customer entity in the system, serving as the primary domain model for all customer-related operations including creation, retrieval, and segmentation.
docs.remarks: A  is the aggregate root for customer data. Every customer is assigned a globally unique  at creation time and must have a non-empty . Customers are active by default and can be deactivated through . Tags enable flexible segmentation—for example, marking customers as "vip", "wholesale", or "beta-tester"—without requiring schema changes. The property controls which channel is used for outbound communications.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC date and time when this customer record was created.
docs.remarks: Automatically set to  at construction. This value should not be modified after the initial persistence operation.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the customer's email address, used for account verification and transactional notifications.
docs.remarks: When , the customer has not provided an email and cannot receive email-based communications. Validation should enforce RFC 5322 format when a value is present.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the customer's full display name.
docs.remarks: This property is required and must not be  or whitespace. It is used throughout the UI and in transactional emails as the primary way to address the customer.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the globally unique identifier for this customer.
docs.remarks: This value is typically generated by the caller at construction time using . It serves as the primary key across all persistence layers and is immutable after creation.

docs.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the customer account is currently active.
docs.remarks: Inactive customers are excluded from search results and cannot place new orders. Use  to set this to in a controlled manner that also raises the appropriate domain events.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the customer's preferred communication channel.
docs.remarks: Defaults to . When sending notifications, the system should honour this preference and fall back to email only when the preferred channel is unavailable.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the list of free-form tags used for customer segmentation and targeted marketing campaigns.
docs.remarks: Tags are case-insensitive string labels. Common conventions include lowercase kebab-case (e.g., "early-adopter", "high-value"). An empty list indicates that no segmentation has been applied.

docs.summary: Represents an inclusive date range defined by a start and end boundary, with helpers for containment checks, overlap detection, and duration calculation.
docs.remarks: is a  struct that implements for efficient, allocation-free value comparisons. The constructor enforces the invariant that  must be on or before , throwing  otherwise. Common use cases include filtering orders by date, defining subscription periods, and computing business-day durations. The  and methods enable calendar-style range queries.

docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the  struct.

parameters.end: The inclusive end date of the range.
parameters.start: The inclusive start date of the range.

docs.summary: Determines whether the specified date falls within this range.
docs.returns: if  is within the range; otherwise, .

docs.summary: Determines whether two  instances are equal.
Determines whether two  instances are not equal.
Determines whether this range overlaps with another range.
docs.returns: if the ranges overlap; otherwise, .

parameters.other: The other date range to test.

docs.summary: Returns a human-readable representation of the date range.
Gets the duration of time between  and .
Gets the inclusive end date of the range.
Gets the inclusive start date of the range.
Feature flags that can be independently enabled for the application.
docs.remarks: This enum uses the  to allow bitwise combination of values. Use  as a convenience mask to enable every flag at once.

enumMembers.name: DarkMode
enumMembers.description: Enables the dark mode user interface.

enumMembers.name: AllFeatures
enumMembers.description: Convenience flag that enables all features.

docs.summary: Defines the contract for managing customer lifecycle operations including creation, retrieval, search, update, and deactivation.
docs.remarks: Implementations of  are responsible for enforcing business rules such as email uniqueness, required-field validation, and the invariant that deactivated customers cannot place new orders. All methods are asynchronous and return  to support non-blocking I/O against the underlying data store. Callers should handle returns from  gracefully, as a missing customer is a normal condition rather than an error.

docs.summary: Persists a new  and returns the created entity with any server-generated values (e.g., timestamps) populated.
docs.returns: A  that resolves to the newly created with  set by the server.

parameters.customer: The customer to create. The  and properties are required.

docs.summary: Marks a customer as inactive, preventing them from placing new orders or appearing in search results.
docs.remarks: Deactivation is a soft operation\u2014the customer record is retained in the data store with  set to . This method is idempotent: calling it on an already-inactive customer returns  without side effects.
docs.returns: if the customer was found and successfully deactivated; if no customer with the given  exists or the customer was already inactive.

parameters.id: The identifier of the customer to deactivate.

docs.summary: Retrieves a single customer by its globally unique identifier.
docs.remarks: This method does not throw when the customer is not found. Callers should check for a  result and return an appropriate HTTP 404 or equivalent response.
docs.returns: The  if a record with the specified exists; otherwise, .

parameters.id: The  identifier of the customer to retrieve.

members.name: SearchAsync
members.signature: Task<IReadOnlyList<Customer>> ICustomerService.SearchAsync(string query, int skip = 0, int take = 25)

docs.summary: Searches for customers whose  or matches the given query string, with support for offset-based pagination.
docs.returns: A read-only list of customers matching the search criteria, ordered by ascending. Returns an empty list when no matches are found.

parameters.query: A case-insensitive search string. The implementation performs a contains-style match against  and .
parameters.skip: The number of matching results to skip, enabling offset-based pagination. Defaults to 0.
parameters.take: The maximum number of results to return per page. Defaults to 25. Values above 100 may be clamped by the implementation.

docs.summary: Replaces the stored customer record with the values from the supplied object.
docs.returns: The updated  as persisted, which may include server-modified fields such as an updated timestamp.

parameters.customer: The customer entity containing the updated values. The must match an existing record.

docs.summary: A service that publishes notifications for key order lifecycle events.
docs.remarks: Consumers subscribe to the events exposed by this interface to react to order state transitions. Implementations are responsible for raising events at the appropriate points in the order processing pipeline.

docs.summary: Occurs when an order has been cancelled.
Occurs when an order has been delivered.
Occurs when a new order has been placed.
Occurs when an order has been shipped.
Publishes a notification that the specified order was cancelled.
Publishes a notification that the specified order was placed.
Publishes a notification that the specified order was shipped.
Defines a generic repository contract for performing CRUD and query operations against a persistent store of  objects.
docs.remarks: abstracts the data-access layer, allowing service classes to operate against an in-memory fake, an EF Core DbContext, or a Dapper-based implementation without code changes. Implementations are responsible for: Opening and closing database connections or scoping units of work.Mapping between CLR entities and the underlying storage representation.Propagating  to all I/O calls.

docs.summary: Represents a monetary amount paired with an ISO 4217 currency code, ensuring that different currencies are never accidentally mixed in arithmetic.
docs.remarks: is a value object implemented as a record struct to guarantee immutability and value-based equality. Using instead of a bare  prevents subtle bugs where values in different currencies are summed or compared. The  is stored as a  to avoid the floating-point precision issues inherent to  in financial calculations.

parameters.Amount: The numeric value of the monetary amount.

docs.summary: Represents a monetary amount paired with an ISO 4217 currency code, ensuring that different currencies are never accidentally mixed in arithmetic.
docs.remarks: is a value object implemented as a record struct to guarantee immutability and value-based equality. Using instead of a bare  prevents subtle bugs where values in different currencies are summed or compared. The  is stored as a  to avoid the floating-point precision issues inherent to  in financial calculations.

parameters.Amount: The numeric value of the monetary amount.

docs.summary: Creates a  value denominated in euros.
docs.returns: A new  instance with currency set to "EUR".

docs.summary: Returns a string representation in the format "100.00 USD".
docs.returns: A formatted string of the monetary value.

docs.summary: Creates a  value denominated in US dollars.
docs.returns: A new  instance with currency set to "USD".

docs.summary: The numeric value of the monetary amount.
The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code (e.g., "USD", "EUR", "GBP").
Represents an order placed by a , containing one or more line items and tracking lifecycle state through .
docs.remarks: An  is always associated with a single customer via . The order progresses through a linear state machine: →  → → . At any point before shipping, the order can transition to . The  property is computed from the sum of each line item's quantity multiplied by its unit price. It is not persisted and is recalculated on every access.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the identifier of the  who placed this order.
docs.remarks: This is a foreign key reference. The referenced customer must exist and be active at the time the order is created.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the globally unique identifier for this order.
Gets or sets the collection of line items that make up this order.
docs.remarks: An order must contain at least one item to be valid. Each item captures a product name, quantity, and unit price at the time of purchase.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC date and time when the order was placed.
Gets or sets the current lifecycle status of the order.
docs.remarks: Defaults to . State transitions should be performed through the order processing pipeline, which validates that each transition is legal and raises the corresponding event via .

docs.summary: Gets the total price of the order, computed as the sum of each item's multiplied by its .
docs.remarks: This is a calculated property and is not stored. It reflects the current state of the  collection.

docs.summary: Provides data for order-related events.
docs.remarks: This class extends  to carry the that triggered the event along with a UTC timestamp indicating when the event occurred.

parameters.order: The order associated with the event.

docs.summary: Provides data for order-related events.
docs.remarks: This class extends  to carry the that triggered the event along with a UTC timestamp indicating when the event occurred.

parameters.order: The order associated with the event.

docs.summary: Gets the order associated with this event.
Gets the UTC timestamp indicating when the event was raised.
Represents a single line item within an , capturing the product, quantity, and price at the time of purchase.
docs.remarks: Prices are captured at order time and do not change if the product's catalogue price is updated later. The line total can be calculated as Quantity * UnitPrice.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the display name of the product being ordered.
docs.remarks: This is a snapshot of the product name at order time. It is stored denormalized so that the order remains readable even if the product is renamed or removed from the catalogue.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the number of units ordered.
docs.remarks: Must be a positive integer. Defaults to 1.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the unit price of the product at the time the order was placed.
docs.remarks: Stored as a  to avoid floating-point rounding errors in financial calculations.

docs.summary: Defines the lifecycle states an  progresses through from initial placement to final fulfilment or cancellation.
docs.remarks: The legal transitions form a linear pipeline with an escape hatch: →  →  → . An order may be moved to at any point before .

enumMembers.name: Pending
enumMembers.description: The order has been created but has not yet been reviewed or confirmed.

enumMembers.name: Confirmed
enumMembers.description: Payment has been verified and the order is approved for fulfilment.

enumMembers.name: Shipped
enumMembers.description: The order has left the warehouse and is in transit to the customer.

enumMembers.name: Delivered
enumMembers.description: The order has been received by the customer, completing the fulfilment cycle.

enumMembers.name: Cancelled
enumMembers.description: The order was cancelled before shipment. Cancelled orders cannot be reinstated.

docs.summary: Represents a single page of results from a paginated query, including the items on the current page and metadata for navigating the full result set.
docs.remarks: is returned by and other paged query methods. The , , and  properties are computed automatically to simplify building pagination controls in UIs and hypermedia links in API responses. When  is zero,  returns zero to avoid division-by-zero errors.

parameters.Items: The read-only list of items on the current page.
parameters.Page: The one-based page number represented by this result.
parameters.PageSize: The maximum number of items per page.
parameters.TotalCount: The total number of items across all pages in the underlying query.

A machine-readable error response body following the RFC 9457 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs) specification, providing a standardized structure that clients can parse without knowledge of the specific API.
docs.remarks: Every field in  is optional. At minimum, set and . Use  to point to a documentation page describing the error category. The dictionary allows attaching arbitrary additional context—common patterns include: A "traceId" for correlating with server logs.A "errors" array of  for 400 responses.A "retryAfter" value for 429 / 503 responses.

docs.summary: Gets or sets a human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
Gets the extension members for this problem details instance.
docs.remarks: Use this dictionary to include additional context such as trace identifiers, validation errors, or retry-after hints.

docs.summary: Gets or sets a URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.
Gets or sets the HTTP status code generated by the origin server for this occurrence.
Gets or sets a short, human-readable summary of the problem type.
Gets or sets a URI reference that identifies the problem type.
A data transfer object for product information, annotated with System.Text.Json serialization attributes to control wire format.
docs.remarks: demonstrates the most common JSON customization patterns supported by System.Text.Json: to map CLR property names to snake_case or camelCase JSON keys. to hide internal-only properties from serialized output. to enforce that a property must be present during deserialization. Properties marked with  will cause to throw a if the corresponding key is missing from the input JSON.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC date and time when the product was first added to the catalogue. Maps to the JSON key "added_at".
Gets or sets the product category used for catalogue organization. Maps to the JSON key "category".
Gets or sets the long-form product description, supporting plain text only. Maps to the JSON key "description".
docs.remarks: When , the product has no description and UIs should display a placeholder or omit the section entirely.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the unique product identifier. This is required during deserialization and maps to the JSON key "id".
An internal tracking code used by the warehouse management system. This property is excluded from JSON serialization via and is never exposed through the API.
Gets or sets whether the product is currently available for purchase. Maps to the JSON key "available".
docs.remarks: When , the product should not appear in storefront listings but may still be visible in the admin catalogue.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the human-readable product name displayed in catalogues and search results. Maps to the JSON key "name".
Gets or sets the unit price in the store's base currency. Maps to the JSON key "price".
docs.remarks: Prices are represented as  to preserve precision in financial calculations. Negative values are not permitted.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the stock keeping unit code for inventory tracking. Maps to the JSON key "sku".
docs.remarks: SKU codes follow the format XX-#### where XX is a two-letter category prefix and #### is a numeric sequence.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the list of searchable tags associated with this product. Maps to the JSON key "tags".
docs.remarks: Tags enable faceted search and filtering. They should be lowercase, hyphen-separated strings (e.g., "usb-c", "wireless").

docs.summary: Configuration options for API rate limiting.
docs.remarks: Bind an instance of  from the application's configuration section (e.g., "RateLimiting") to control how many requests a client may make within a sliding time window.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of requests allowed within the time window.
Gets or sets the number of seconds a client should wait before retrying after being rate-limited.
Gets or sets the duration of the sliding window in seconds.
Encapsulates the outcome of a domain operation as either a success carrying a value of type , or a failure carrying an error message.
docs.remarks: implements the discriminated-result pattern: callers inspect  (or ) before accessing  or . Accessing on a failed result returns  for . Prefer the static factory methods  and  over direct construction to ensure the success/error invariants are satisfied. This type can be used as a method return value in service layers to avoid throwing exceptions for expected failure conditions.

docs.summary: Creates a failed result with the specified error message.
docs.returns: A  representing a failed outcome.

docs.summary: Creates a successful result containing the specified value.
docs.returns: A  representing a successful outcome.

docs.summary: Gets the error message describing why the operation failed.
Gets a value indicating whether the operation failed.
Gets a value indicating whether the operation succeeded.
Gets the value produced by a successful operation.
Base class for entities that support soft deletion in addition to audit tracking.
docs.remarks: Entities derived from  are never physically removed from the data store. Instead, the  flag is set to and  records the time of deletion. Query logic should filter out soft-deleted entities by default.

docs.summary: Restores a previously soft-deleted entity, clearing deletion metadata.
Marks the entity as deleted by setting  to and recording the current UTC time and the identity of the user performing the deletion.

parameters.deletedBy: The identifier of the user performing the deletion.

docs.summary: Gets or sets the UTC date and time when the entity was soft-deleted.
Gets or sets the identifier of the user who deleted the entity.
Gets or sets a value indicating whether the entity has been soft-deleted.
Describes a single validation error tied to a specific input field, including a human-readable message and an optional machine-readable error code.
docs.remarks: Validation errors are typically collected during request processing and surfaced to the client as an array within a response. Use consistent error codes across the API to allow clients to build locale-aware error displays.

parameters.Code: An application-specific error code for programmatic handling, such as "INVALID_FORMAT" or "FIELD_REQUIRED". When , the client should rely on the  for context.
parameters.Field: The name of the input field that failed validation, using the same casing as the JSON property name (e.g., "email", "fullName").
parameters.Message: A human-readable, locale-sensitive message describing why the field value is invalid (e.g., "Email address is not in a valid format.").
